painpoints.title: Cross-SKU Pain Points
painpoints.desc: Aggregates the 'cons' themes from all 20 reviewed SKUs into category-level buckets. Ranked by # of SKUs affected to surface systemic issues across the whole category.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.intro: Multi-dimensional research for the US pet dog food and treats market. Every figure carries a source link and fetch timestamp.
dashboard.aovByCatDesc: Based on Amazon BSR pet food + Chewy + DTC current prices.
dashboard.marketSizeNote: Pet food & treats ~$58 B; dog food is 60%+ of pet food spend.
dashboard.fact1Body: Mass-market kibble on Amazon $45–$85/30 lb; premium grain-free $70–$120; DTC fresh subs $2 trial → $3–5/day.
dashboard.fact2Body: Impulse buys <$12 (Milk-Bone, Pup-Peroni); dental chews $30–$45 (Greenies leads); subscription boxes $29–$39/mo.
dashboard.fact3Body: Amazon S&S gives 5–15% off recurring kibble/treat orders; Chewy Autoship up to 35% off on 5th order — core retention play.
dashboard.fact4Body: Q4 holiday gifting + new-pet adoption peak; January 'health resolution' food switches; summer cold-chain costs tick up for fresh.

dogFood.title: Dog Food
dogFood.desc: Dry kibble (Amazon BSR) + premium (Chewy) + fresh DTC subscriptions — with SKU, supplier & wholesale contacts.

dogTreats.title: Dog Treats
dogTreats.desc: Dental chews, training treats, biscuits & subscription boxes — with SKU, supplier & wholesale contacts.

seasonality.title: Seasonality
seasonality.desc: US dog food demand peaks Oct–Jan (holiday stock-up + diet switches); summer is softer; November BFCM has the deepest discounts.

influencers.title: Influencers
influencers.desc: YouTube / TikTok / Reddit dog nutrition & lifestyle creators — kibble reviews, treat unboxings, and r/Dogfood community.

logistics.title: Logistics
logistics.desc: Door-to-door comparison: ship-from-China vs US warehouse (Amazon FBA / Chewy / cold-chain fresh); including competitor free-shipping thresholds.
logistics.thresholdNote: Pet food norms are $0 (Prime/subscription) or $35–$49 (Chewy/Petco). Large kibble bags retain via Subscribe & Save and Autoship — not high free-shipping thresholds.

methodology.title: Methodology & Sources
methodology.desc: Every data point on this site carries a source link + fetch timestamp; multi-source cross-validation; deltas >15% flagged.
methodology.loopBody: Review pros / cons run on Cursor — no third-party LLM key required. The scraper merges raw Amazon (Rainforest) + DTC (Yotpo/Shopify/Trustpilot) + Reddit (PRAW) reviews per SKU, then either: (a) calls Cursor SDK Agent.prompt(...) directly with your CURSOR_API_KEY for cron-friendly automation, or (b) queues every SKU to _insights_pending.md so you can ask the Cursor IDE chat to batch-process them. Both modes use the same prompt + JSON schema and your existing Cursor subscription — zero extra spend.
methodology.dataQualityVerifiedDesc: Product name, ASIN/SKU, current price, MSRP, BSR rank, shipping policy, pet-food seasonality prior (APPA 2025), Zak George / Rachel Fusaro subscriber counts (verified via YouTube official pages).
methodology.dataQualityPendingDesc: Rating, review count, Reddit subscriber count: shown as an ⌛ pending-scrape chip in the UI. The cheapest verified path is Rainforest API (100 requests/month free, no card required) — drop the key into scraper/.env then run python verify_asins.py for a seed-vs-live side-by-side table. Or use Amazon PA-API / YouTube API / Reddit API through python run_all.py.
methodology.dataQualityNote: To avoid misleading numbers, the initial seed leaves unverified fields explicitly empty (marked ‘pending scrape’) rather than filling placeholder values. Run the scraper before quoting those fields in commercial decisions.
stackItems: Frontend: Vite + React + TypeScript + Tailwind + Recharts + i18next
Data layer: static JSON (web/data/*.json), populated by Python scrapers
Sources: Amazon Best Sellers (Playwright), Shopify Storefront API, YouTube Data API v3, Reddit (PRAW), Google Trends
limitItems: Amazon anti-bot: residential proxies or PA-API credentials required
TikTok has no official API; third-party service needed (disabled by default)
Reddit sampling only; not full sentiment coverage
